texto: And I read hundreds and thousands of books, day and night, always awake and always eager to seek health. But in no book I found what I was looking for. Then, shut up in my parents' house, I thought and suffered for hundreds and thousands of hours, always awake and always mindful of the tremendous anxiety of health. But I still have not found what I was looking for.
explicacao: Citação de Giovanni Papini sobre temas ligados a saude mental. “And I read hundreds and thousands of books, day and night, always awake and always eager to seek …” articula saude-mental, health, suffering em registro de saude mental, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

frase_original: And I read hundreds and thousands of books, day and night, always awake and always eager to seek health. But in no book I found what I was looking for. Then, shut up in my parents' house, I thought and suffered for hundreds and thousands of hours, always awake and always mindful of the tremendous anxiety of health. But I still have not found what I was looking for.

texto: Life to be bearable must be lived intensely. Through it a continuous stream of emotion passes. Though that emotion is ever changing as flowing water changes, it at least bears us along on a current that gives the illusion of continuity and permanence. But analyze life, tear its trappings off, lay it bare with thought, with logic, with philosophy, and its emptiness is revealed as a bottomless pit; its nothingness frankly confesses to nothingness, and Despair comes to perch in the soul.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Giovanni Papini sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. A afirmação “Life to be bearable must be lived intensely. Through it a continuous stream of emotion passes. Th…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, depression, despair, útil em momentos de mudanca e despair.
